Loading rules: Add the missing using directive for the namespace that contains RtfLoadOptions: `using Aspose.Words.Loading;`.
Saving/Export rules: When the target format is Markdown, call Document.Save with SaveFormat.Markdown (or .md extension).
Images/Shapes/OLE rules: Add required using directives for Aspose.Words and Aspose.Words.Drawing.
Use NodeType.Shape to locate the shape node.
Copy required formatting from the old shape to the new shape after insertion.
To set a custom caption for an OLE object, use DocumentBuilder.InsertOleObjectAsIcon overload that accepts an icon stream and a caption string.
InsertOleObjectAsIcon returns a Shape; use that Shape to access OleFormat for further modifications (ProgId, IsLocked, etc.).
Keep the OLE object embedded by passing isLinked = false.
When you need a custom caption for an OLE icon, either pass it to InsertOleObjectAsIcon or set OleFormat.OlePackage.DisplayName after insertion.
Do not call Shape.GetAncestorNode – that method does not exist. Use the index from the NodeCollection or maintain a separate counter.
Iterate over the NodeCollection returned by Document.GetChildNodes(NodeType.Shape, true) to guarantee all shapes are visited.
Keep the rest of the logic unchanged; the code already correctly iterates over Shape nodes and saves embedded OLE objects.
If you prefer not to use RtfLoadOptions, you can simply instantiate the Document with `new Document(rtfPath)`, because OLE data is preserved by default for RTF.
